methods.description: The process involves chemical treatment of subsamples for taxonomic identification and analysis of pollen grains using microscopy.

steps.description: The standard chemical treatment of the subsamples (one sample = 8 cm 3 ) involved treatment with hydrochloric acid, hydrofluoric acid, potassium hydroxide, and acetolysis, followed by filtration through 250-µm mesh.
steps.searchable_summary: apply chemical treatment and filter subsamples

steps.description: The subsamples were mounted on slides in glycerol jelly and examined under light microscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: mount subsamples on slides and examine under microscope

steps.description: Taxonomic identification was based on the pollen reference collection and reference pollen catalogs.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen using reference collections and catalogs

steps.description: Taxonomic ordination of orders was based on APG IV. Taxonomic names followed the Missouri Botanical Garden nomenclature.
steps.searchable_summary: ordinate and name taxa using APG IV and MOBOT nomenclature

steps.description: A minimum of 300 angiosperm pollen grains was counted for each subsample, with this number determined using saturation curves.
steps.searchable_summary: count pollen grains and determine number using saturation curves

steps.description: For the taxonomic pollen descriptions, the morphological nomenclature was based on Punt et al. (2007).
steps.searchable_summary: describe pollen morphology using Punt et al. nomenclature

steps.description: Photomicrographs were captured using a digital camera connected to a light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: capture photomicrographs using digital camera and microscope
